meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Learn how a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code help you launch your MVP faster. A beginner-level case study.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project abandonment is 'infrastructure fatigue.' By utilizing a robust SaaS starter kit, you offload authentication, billing, and database setup to battle-tested boilerplate. This allows you to focus exclusively on your core value proposition, reducing time-to-market from months to days and ensuring your MVP actually reaches production.
content.intro:
You have a graveyard of half-finished GitHub repositories. You start with high-octane motivation, designing elegant architectures on a whiteboard, only to drown in the minutiae of setting up database schemas, Stripe webhooks, and authentication flows. The spark dies, and the project becomes another 'what-if' in your terminal history.

But the most successful SaaS founders don't build from scratch; they leverage leverage. By utilizing a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you bypass the soul-crushing boilerplate phase. This isn't about cutting corners; it's about architectural velocity. Today, we bridge the gap between 'idea' and 'revenue' using the synergy of Antigravity's rapid scaffolding and Claude Code's autonomous execution.

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using the Antigravity starter kit to ensure a production-ready environment with pre-configured auth and database connectivity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS boilerplate with Next.js, PostgreSQL, and Stripe integration. Include role-based access control and a clean dashboard layout.

steps.title: Define Schema with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate your business logic into a concrete Prisma schema. This ensures data integrity from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a SaaS subscription service with User, Project, and Subscription models. Ensure relational integrity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate prisma schema for User, Project, and Subscription models with proper relationships.'

steps.title: Configure Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Handle payments without the manual overhead. Use Claude Code to map subscription events to your database status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler that updates the user subscription status in the database when a payment succeeds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Create a Next.js API route for Stripe webhooks to handle customer.subscription.updated events.'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'secret sauce'—the unique feature that makes your SaaS valuable. Keep the code modular.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function that processes user input for my core product feature, ensuring input validation and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Refactor the core logic into a service-based architecture within the /services directory.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your MVP to a staging environment to validate the flow with real users immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration and a checklist for pre-launch production sanity checks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Run build and test suite, then provide a production-readiness checklist for Vercel deployment.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development; if you can't charge for it, the feature isn't an MVP priority.
Never write raw SQL; keep your schema strictly managed by an ORM to prevent migration hell.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth flows before you write a single line of UI.
Adopt a 'Ship-Every-Friday' cadence to prevent feature creep and maintain founder momentum.
Keep your database schema lean; YAGNI (You Ain't Gonna Need It) applies to database columns just as much as code.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, deploy your landing page, set up a feedback loop via email collection, and iterate based on actual user behavior rather than your assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a folder. Leverage the best tools to ship faster, monetize sooner, and finally finish what you start. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
